title: Goodyera discoidea
taxonomy: Goodyera discoidea is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Goodyerae. This species is further classified as part of the genus Goodyera, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Goodyera discoidea is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape and inflorescence structure.

intro: Goodyera discoidea, also known as the Rattlesnake Plantain or Pacific Rattlesnake Plantain, is a perennial orchid native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Goodyera discoidea include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects.
